content:
# About Shashank Chakrawarty

Shashank Chakrawarty is a data engineering and cloud analytics specialist with over 4 years of experience building scalable data pipelines, cloud-native analytics workflows, real-time and near-real-time processing systems, and governed data platforms.

He has worked across GCP, Azure, and AWS, with hands-on exposure to BigQuery, Cloud Composer, Apache Airflow, Azure Databricks, Spark, PySpark, Delta Lake, Redshift, Glue, EMR, Athena, Snowflake, dbt, Kafka, and CDC-style ingestion patterns.

His strongest profile areas include BigQuery optimization, Airflow orchestration, Spark-based processing, Azure Databricks migration work, SEO and SEM analytics pipelines, API-based data integration, data governance, and stakeholder-facing analytics engineering.
He is a strong fit for Data Engineer and Senior Data Engineer roles that require cloud data pipelines, platform ownership, optimization, real-time analytics, and measurable business impact.

# Athena Query Layer Usage

Athena is part of Shashank's AWS analytics stack. This supports query-on-data-lake and serverless analytics conversations, especially when recruiters ask about AWS familiarity in data engineering roles.

# AWS Project Context

His AWS profile is relevant to data engineering and analytics use cases such as warehousing, ETL, distributed processing, and query-layer analytics.

Use S3 for storage and staging, Glue for ETL, EMR for distributed processing, Redshift for warehouse-style analytics, Athena for query access, and Lambda for lightweight serverless workflow support when explaining his AWS familiarity.

# AWS Services Used

Shashank's portfolio and resume explicitly include AWS, Redshift, S3, Glue, EMR, Lambda, and Athena as part of his broader cloud data engineering stack.

These services should be presented as relevant AWS data engineering exposure, with strongest proven depth in multi-cloud data pipeline work rather than pure AWS-only specialization.

# AWS vs GCP Experience

Shashank's strongest recent hands-on depth is in GCP, especially BigQuery, orchestration, and analytics workflows at Forbes Advisor. He also has strong Azure Databricks and Spark depth from The Modern Data Company, and relevant AWS data engineering exposure covering Redshift, Glue, EMR, Athena, S3, and Lambda.

A recruiter-safe positioning is that he is strongest in GCP and Azure, while still being relevant and credible in AWS data engineering contexts.

# Azure Databricks Experience

Azure Databricks is one of the strongest platforms in Shashank's profile outside GCP. At The Modern Data Company, he worked with Azure Databricks, Spark, Delta Lake, Synapse, ADLS Gen2, and migration-oriented data engineering workflows.

This supports strong answers around distributed processing, Spark optimization, migration, and enterprise data platform modernization.

# Behavioral Stories

## Performance optimization He improved query performance by up to 35 percent while driving 20 percent cost savings through optimization and cloud data engineering improvements.

## Automation impact He eliminated 40 percent of manual effort and improved campaign effectiveness by 50 percent through automated data pipelines.

## Governance impact He improved data integrity by 20 percent and reduced GDPR violations by 95 percent through stronger governance standards.

## Mentoring and communication His profile highlights recognition from stakeholders for translating complex technical solutions into clear business outcomes.

# dbt Experience

Shashank's profile explicitly includes Data Build Tool and dbt in his top skills and orchestration stack. A grounded explanation is that dbt fits into his SQL transformation, analytics engineering, and maintainable data workflow experience.

# EMR and Glue Batch Processing

Shashank's AWS exposure includes EMR and Glue in data engineering contexts. This supports recruiter conversations around batch processing, ETL workflows, distributed processing, and cloud-native analytics engineering.

Keep answers factual and scoped to the technologies explicitly listed in his portfolio and curated knowledge.

# Forbes Advisor Case Study

## Role Data Engineer, June 2024 to present.

## Summary At Forbes Advisor, Shashank engineered a scalable SEO data pipeline that improved ingestion efficiency by 40 percent and improved SEO analytics accuracy by 25 percent. He optimized BigQuery performance to reduce execution time by 30 percent, enabling near-real-time analytics. He also implemented real-time Slack alerts that improved team response time by 20 percent and enhanced Google Analytics stream and batch processing to reduce data processing duration by 35 percent.

## Technologies BigQuery, Cloud Composer, Apache Airflow, Python, Google Analytics, stream and batch processing, Slack alerting.
## Good questions this answers What is his current role? Has he worked with BigQuery in production? Has he built near-real-time analytics pipelines? Has he worked with Airflow and GCP?

# Interview FAQ

## Tell me about yourself Shashank is a Data Engineering and Cloud Analytics Specialist with over 4 years of experience across GCP, Azure, and AWS, focused on scalable data pipelines, cloud-native architectures, real-time analytics platforms, and governed data engineering solutions.

## What are his strongest technologies? Python, SQL, BigQuery, Apache Airflow, Spark, Azure Databricks, Delta Lake, dbt, Snowflake, Redshift, and cloud data engineering across GCP, Azure, and AWS.

## Has he worked with BigQuery and Airflow? Yes. His Forbes Advisor work includes BigQuery optimization and Airflow-based orchestration.

## Has he worked with Azure Databricks? Yes. His Modern Data Company experience includes Azure Databricks, Spark, Delta Lake, and migration work.
## Has he worked with AWS? Yes. His profile includes Redshift, S3, Glue, EMR, Lambda, and Athena in data engineering and analytics contexts.

# The Modern Data Company Case Study

## Role Senior Analytics Engineer, January 2022 to June 2024.

## Summary At The Modern Data Company, Shashank integrated multi-platform enterprise data, delivering 70 percent ROI and improving processing efficiency by 30 percent through infrastructure migration. He led API to CRM integration that reduced manual data entry by 40 percent. He optimized Spark performance and strengthened data security, improving efficiency by 35 percent and reducing compliance incidents by 35 percent. He also implemented data governance frameworks that increased customer engagement and retention by 30 percent.

## Technologies Azure Databricks, Spark, PySpark, Delta Lake, Azure Data Factory, Synapse, ADLS Gen2, Azure SQL Database, Redshift.

# Project: CDC with Kafka and Delta

Shashank's profile includes Kafka exposure and CDC-oriented modern data engineering patterns. His broader experience includes Spark, distributed processing, and cloud-native pipeline design, making him relevant for roles that require event-driven ingestion and scalable data movement.

Note: only discuss CDC, Kafka, or Delta Lake at the level supported by the portfolio knowledge and curated resume content.

content:
# Project: Google Analytics and GA4 Pipelines

Shashank worked on Google Analytics stream and batch processing that reduced processing duration by 35 percent. This demonstrates hands-on experience with event-oriented analytics pipelines, cloud-native processing, and downstream reporting support.

Relevant technologies include Google Analytics, BigQuery, and orchestration workflows around stream and batch processing.

metadata.title: Project: Google Analytics and GA4 Pipelines

content:
# Project: Real-Time SEO Pipeline

At Forbes Advisor, Shashank built a scalable SEO data pipeline that improved ingestion efficiency by 40 percent and analytics accuracy by 25 percent. He also optimized BigQuery queries by 30 percent to support near-real-time analytics for SEO reporting and decision-making.

# Project: SEM Conversion Upload Pipelines

Shashank has architected and implemented SEM conversion upload pipelines across advertising platforms including Google, Bing, Facebook, TikTok, and Reddit. The work involves ingestion, transformation, platform-specific routing, validation, monitoring, and business-critical pipeline reliability.

This project demonstrates strong API integration, marketing data engineering, orchestration, and operational ownership.

# Redshift Migration and Analytics

Redshift is part of Shashank's cloud data warehouse exposure. His profile supports discussion of warehouse-style analytics, SQL-driven reporting, migration-minded platform thinking, and cross-platform experience across BigQuery, Redshift, Synapse, Trino, and Snowflake.

# RTB Analytica Case Study

## Role Data Engineer, July 2021 to January 2022.

## Summary At RTB Analytica, Shashank automated multi-source data pipelines involving Facebook Graph API, Klaviyo, and Google data, cutting processing time by 40 percent. He drove cross-functional data consolidation that reduced end-to-end processing time by 25 percent and built enterprise-grade data quality monitoring that reduced data errors by 40 percent.

## Technologies Python, Airflow, Facebook Graph API, Klaviyo, Google Analytics, data quality monitoring.

# Skills Matrix

## Core technologies Python, SQL, PySpark, Spark SQL, Apache Airflow, BigQuery, Azure Databricks, Delta Lake, dbt, Snowflake, Kafka.

## GCP BigQuery, Cloud Composer, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Cloud Functions, Google Analytics processing, scalable SEO and SEM analytics workflows.

## Azure Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Synapse, ADLS Gen2, Delta Lake, Spark optimization, infrastructure migration, governed data engineering workflows.

## AWS Redshift, S3, Glue, EMR, Lambda, Athena for data engineering and analytics contexts.

## Typical recruiter search terms covered bigquery, airflow, spark, pyspark, snowflake, dbt, databricks, azure databricks, aws, redshift, glue, emr, athena, kafka, cdc, ga4, seo pipeline, sem conversion uploads, real-time pipeline.

# Snowflake Experience

Snowflake is explicitly listed in Shashank's skills. The strongest safe positioning is that Snowflake is part of his broader cloud data warehouse and analytics engineering toolkit alongside BigQuery, Redshift, Synapse, and Trino.

Use this to answer recruiter questions about warehouse breadth, SQL transformation environments, and multi-platform analytics engineering.
